name: Michigan (United States)
description: A blue field bearing the Michigan state coat of arms in the centre. The arms feature a shield with a man on a peninsula at sunrise, supported by a moose and an elk, surmounted by a bald eagle holding an olive branch and arrows, with the mottos 'TUEBOR', 'E PLURIBUS UNUM', and 'SI QUAERIS PENINSULAM AMOENAM CIRCUMSPICE'.
history: Michigan adopted its first state flag in 1837, modeled on regimental colours used by Michigan troops in the Mexican-American and Civil Wars. The current design — the state coat of arms on a plain blue field — was adopted by the legislature on 1 August 1911.

features.role: the Michigan state coat of arms
features.symbolism: shield with a man on a peninsula, sun rising over a lake, and the motto 'TUEBOR' ('I will defend'); supported by a moose and an elk; crested by a bald eagle holding olive branch and arrows; banner below reads 'SI QUAERIS PENINSULAM AMOENAM CIRCUMSPICE' ('If you seek a pleasant peninsula, look about you'); above the eagle the motto 'E PLURIBUS UNUM'
